TD_1_1:
Welcome to <orange>Godius</orange>! Here's a guide to <orange>creating your character</orange>. 

Your <green>character name</green> may <green>only include letters from supported languages</green> and <green>numbers</green>, and is subject to a <red>character limit</red>. Select your <green>gender</green> and customize a unique character with <green>20 hairstyles</green> and <green>9 hair colors</green>.
TD_1_2:
Your <green>class</green> cannot be changed once selected, so please choose carefully. Please decide on both your <green>main class</green> and <green>secondary class</green> together.

Your <green>main class</green> determines how you fight in the world of Godius, while your <green>secondary class</green> allows you to learn skills that can support you in combat or enrich your activities outside of it.
TD_1_3:
The <green>stats</green> you should focus on will vary depending on <green>your class</green>, so please choose carefully. 

<green>Warriors and Gladiators</green> should focus on <orange>STR</orange> and <orange>VIT</orange>, while <green>Mages and Clerics</green> should focus on <orange>VIT</orange>, <orange>MEN</orange>, and <orange>INT</orange>. It is recommended to bring your <green>key stats</green> as close to <red>15</red> as possible.
TD_1_4:
Godius is divided between <orange>two nations</orange>. 

Click the <green>capital city</green> of your desired nation <green>on the map</green> to make your selection. You are free to explore the entire world of Godius regardless of which nation you choose. However, please be aware that players from opposing nations will <red>be treated as enemies</red>.
TD_2_1:
Before your adventure begins, let's go over some <orange>basic controls</orange>. 

Characters <green>below level 6</green> cannot leave the city. Head to the <green>Training Ground</green> to learn the basics and <green>level up</green>! Follow the <green>guide line</green> on the ground to make your way to the <green>Training Ground</green>.
(If you continue with the tutorial, you will receive a reward item step by step.)
TD_2_2:
You can <green>move</green> your character using the arrow <green>key</green>s, <green>WASD</green>, or ,<green>right-click</green>.

Press <green>Enter</green> to <green>chat</green>. You can also switch your <green>chat mode</green> by selecting one of the <green>5 option buttons</green> at the top of the chat window (from left to right: <green>General, Guild, Whisper, Party, and Alliance</green>)
TD_3_1:
You've entered the <green>Training Ground</green>! Don't worry — this is a safe <green>Guard Zone</green>.

<green>Guard Zones</green> are located at the entrances of each map and serve as <green>safe zones</green> where you are protected from enemy and monster attacks. 
<green>Guards</green> are categorized into <green>three colors</green> based on the map's characteristics: <gray>Gray</gray> for <orange>Towns</orange>, <red>Red</red> for <orange>PK Zones</orange>, and <blue>Blue</blue> for <orange>Non-PK Zones</orange>.
TD_3_2:
The <green>inventory</green> can be opened by pressing the<green>' (apostrophe)</green> key or clicking the <green>backpack icon</green> in the main UI. 

<green>Right-clicking</green> an <green>equipment item</green> in your <green>inventory</green> will <green>equip it immediately</green>. Alternatively, you can open the <green>equipment window</green> using the <green>'/'</green> key or the <green>Equip icon</green> below the inventory icon, then <green>left-click</green> an item and move it to the desired <green>equipment slot</green> to equip it.
TD_3_3:
There is a <green>weight limit</green> for <green>equippable items</green>, and your carrying capacity is affected by your <green>STR</green> stat.

<green>Equipment items</green> have <green>durability</green>. A <red>warning appears</red> when durability drops <red>below 10%</red>, and the item becomes <red>unusable</red> at <red>0%</red>. Items with <green>0 durability</green> require <green>additional restoration costs</green>, so we recommend repairing them in advance using <green>oil items</green>.
TD_3_4:
Finally, here's a quick guide to the <orange>basic controls</orange> you'll need for training. 

Press the <green>Space Bar</green> or <green>left-click</green> to perform a <green>basic attack</green>. 

Press the <green>']'</green> key or click the <green>character info icon</green> to check your <green>character's level</green>, <green>experience</green>, and other basic information.
TD_3_5:
<green>Items dropped on the field</green> can be <green>picked up</green> immediately by <green>left-clicking</green>. 

When your inventory is open, you can <green>left-click</green> an item to move it directly into your inventory.
<green>Moving your character over 'LANS'(currency)</green> dropped on the field will <green>automatically pick it up</green>.
TD_4_1:
You've already reached level 6! Here are the <orange>key facilities</orange> you'll be visiting often. 

<green>Click the Map Board object</green> near the city entrance or <green>press</green> the '<green>Home' key</green> to <green>open the map</green>. 

When accessed from the <green>city</green>, it displays the <green>city map</green>. When accessed from the <green>field</green>, it displays the <green>full continent map</green>.
TD_4_2:
Click the <green>Trade Master</green> to enter the <green>Item Exchange</green>, where you can <green>trade items</green> with other players. 

Click the <green>mailbox object</green> or the <green>envelope icon</green> in the top-left of the main UI to open the <green>mail menu</green>. 

The <green>Garrison</green> is a place where you can <green>freely battle</green> other players.
TD_4_3:
<green>Consumable items</green> can be used immediately by <green>right-clicking</green>, and items registered to <green>quick slots 1–9</green> can be used with the corresponding <green>number keys</green>. 

<green>With the inventory open</green>, click the <green>inventory icon</green> once more to switch it to the <green>trash icon</green>, which allows you to <green>delete consumable items</green>. (<orange>Left-click to delete 1 / Ctrl + Left-click to delete all of the same type</orange>) Please note that <green>equipment items</green> <red>cannot be deleted</red>.
TD_4_4:
Click the <green>Storage Box</green> object inside the <green>Storage building</green> to open your <green>personal storage</green>. The <green>Locker</green> is a separate compact storage option. It can hold up to <green>99 items of the same type</green>, limited to consumables of <green>2×2 size or smaller</green>.

Visit the <green>Bank building</green> and speak with the <green>Bank Manager</green> NPC to <green>deposit or withdraw LANS</green>.  If the <green>LANS</green> in your inventory reaches <green>99,999,999 or more</green>, the excess will be <green>automatically transferred</green> to your bank account.
TD_4_5:
Each time you <green>level up</green>, you'll receive <green>Orbs of Power</green>, which can be used to raise your stats (<green>STR, AGI, DEX, INT, VIT, MEN</green>) or <green>increase skill function levels</green>. The higher your stats and function levels become, the <green>more Orbs of Power</green> will be <green>required</green>. 

Parameters such as <green>WC, AC, HC, DC, HP, MP, and SP</green> increase upon <green>leveling up</green>, <green>raising stats</green>, or <green>equipping gear</green>.
TD_4_6:
<green>Skill function levels</green> can be raised up to a <green>maximum of 30</green> using two methods. 

<orange>Chance-based</orange> — Use <green>1 Orb of Power</green> to attempt a <green>+1 function level increase</green>. Success is <red>not guaranteed</red>. 
<orange>Guaranteed</orange> — Spend <green>2 to 15 Orbs of Power</green> to raise the function level with <green>100% success</green>.
TD_4_7:
<orange>Learning skills</orange> will make your combat experience far more dynamic! Here's how to <orange>acquire and use</orange> them. 

Enter the <green>Guild building</green> corresponding to your <green>main or secondary class</green> and speak with the <green>Guild Keeper</green> NPC to learn a variety of skills. Follow the <green>guide line</green> to find your <green>main class guild building</green>.
TD_4_8:
Once learned, <green>magic skills</green> can be moved from the <green>Skill Menu (shortcut key Z)</green> to the <green>magic skill slots</green> in the top-left of the main UI. Use the <green>F5–F9 keys</green> for quick access during combat. 

Most <green>special skills</green> for <green>Warriors and Gladiators</green> are <green>automatically acquired</green> as your <green>function level rises</green>. Each <green>skill</green> has a designated keyboard <orange>shortcut</orange> assigned to it.
TD_4_9:
Before heading to the field, here's a quick overview of the <orange>hunting grounds</orange>.

The recommended <green>starter hunting grounds</green> for '<green>Taferi</green>' are <green>Lucrecia Swampland and Lucrecia Underground Path 1–3</green>. For '<green>Velk</green>', they are <green>Lampas Swampland and Lampas Underground Path 1–3</green>.
TD_5_1:
You’ve finally made it to the field! Let me guide you through the <orange>status ailments</orange> you need to watch out for in the hunting grounds. 

When affected by a <green>status ailment</green>, an icon will<red> appear</red> in the bottom right of the main UI. (From left to right: <orange>Poison, Disease, Hold, and Bind</orange>) <green>Poison</green> and <green>Disease</green> inflict <red>damage over time</red>, while <green>Bind</green> <red>prevents movement</red>, and <green>Hold</green> <red>disables attacks and magic</red>.
TD_5_2:
<green>Diseases</green> and <green>poisons</green> can be cured with <green>potions</green> or <green>instantly removed</green> using the <green>Release</green> skill.

<green>Blocking narrow paths</green> like building entrances for <green>too long</green> will cause '<orange>Petrification</orange>' and forcibly send you to the <green>graveyard</green>. You can also be petrified by monsters. This status can be removed by using a <green>cure potion</green> or <green>by staying logged in for 30 minutes</green>.
TD_5_3:
You're stronger together! Here's how to form a <orange>party</orange> with other players. 

Approach another character within <green>1 tile</green> and press <green>Ctrl + Right-click</green> to send a <green>party request</green>. Once <green>accepted</green>, the <green>party</green> is formed and the <green>requesting player becomes the party leader</green>. Party formation with characters <green>from opposing nations is not available</green>.
TD_5_4:
<green>Once a party is formed</green>, the <green>party window</green> will open in the upper-right area of the screen, where you can <green>monitor your party members' status</green> in real time. 

When <green>hunting in a party</green>, <green>experience</green> gained from defeating monsters is <green>distributed among all party members</green>. Characters with <green>higher levels</green> will <green>receive a greater share</green> of the experience.
TD_6_1:
Welcome back to the city! Let's go over the <orange>shops</orange> and <orange>trading options</orange> available to you. 

Each type of item can only be <green>purchased</green> at its <green>designated specialty shop</green>. However, you can <green>sell items</green> at <green>any shop</green>. <green>Approach another character</green> and press <green>Ctrl + Left-click</green> to send a direct <green>1:1 trade</green> request.
TD_6_2:
Some buildings do not sell items but offer <orange>special services</orange>.

<orange>Blacksmith</orange> — <green>Repair</green> and <green>enhance</green> your <green>equipment</green> here.
<orange>Inn</orange> — Change your <green>appearance</green> or browse <green>costume</green> options.
<orange>Tavern</orange> — Exchange <green>coins</green>, <green>contribution points</green>, and <green>dungeon entry tickets</green>.
TD_6_3:
<green>Enhancing</green> your <green>equipment</green> is one of the best ways to grow stronger! (<orange>Applies only if your secondary craft is in a related field.</orange>) 

<green>Blacksmiths</green> can <green>enhance weapons</green> through the <green>Blacksmith Guild Keeper</green>. <green>Tailors</green> can <green>enhance clothing</green> through the <green>Tailor Guild Keeper</green>. <green>Armor</green> can be <green>enhanced </green>through the <green>Blacksmith NPC at the Blacksmith</green>.
TD_6_4:
Each type of <green>equipment</green> requires <green>different currency</green> and <green>materials</green> for <green>enhancement</green>. 

<green>Equipment</green> can be <green>enhanced</green> up to a <green>maximum of 9 levels</green>, but enhancements at higher levels carry <red>a risk of failure</red>. 

<green>A failed enhancement</green> may result in <red>a level decrease</red> or <red>the permanent destruction</red> of the item — please proceed with caution.

UI_EVENTMENU18X6_F54_138: List for Sale on the Marketplace <white>1 time</white>
UI_EVENTMENU18X6_F63_141: Hi! I am <white>Lucky</white>! I am collecting <white>[Lucky Coins]</white>, but monsters are hiding them! Bring me the <white>[Lucky Coins]</white> monsters have, and I will <white>exchange</white> them for useful <white>items</white>!
UI_EVENTMENU18X6_NEW_F28_01: *The Christmas tree is decorated only when each item reaches 100%. *For the 6 Christmas event item types, you can participate if you own at least one item. *Click Join Event to use all event items in your bag on the Christmas tree. *Event items already at 100% are also used and counted as event participation.
UI_EVENTMENU18X6_NEW_F31_01: The weather has been so hot lately! How can the saponin in my noble body activate like this? Hey, passerby, are you not hot too? I have a nice <white>deal</white> for you. Bring me <white>chicken</white> and <white>broth</white> from the general shop, and for a small fee I will give you restorative <white>samgyetang</white>! Where do I cook it? Some things are better left unknown!
UI_EVENTMENU18X6_NEW_F34_03:
Count / Cost 
1 time 1,000 
2 times 50,000 
3 times 100,000 
4 times 250,000 
5 times 500,000
UI_EVENTMENU18X6_NEW_F42_01: It is a fiery summer, just like my body. On days like this, do you not crave something <white>sweet, tangy</white>, and cold? I knew it, so I prepared some special <white>hot summer desserts</white>! Bring the <white>ingredients</white>, and I will make them myself. You want me to gather them? No. The <white>UV rays</white> outside the parasol are too strong! My skin is delicate, so hurry and bring them~
UI_EVENTMENU18X6_NEW_F46_04: *Items already exchanged cannot be changed. *The item granted matches the exchanging character's gender. *Exchange items cannot be converted back into coupons.
UI_EVENTMENU18X6_NEW_F49_01: Hero, please help me. While escorting the dead to the afterlife, monsters attacked me, and I lost the souls... Bring me the <white>[Souls of the Dead]</white> bound to monsters, and I will exchange them for useful items.

UI_GUILDMENU8X6_F92_179: A <white>Guild Point</white> <white>cost is charged</white>, and if the other party does not accept, or if you <white>cancel</white> the <white>alliance request</white> yourself, you can receive the cost back.
UI_GUILDMENU8X6_NEW_F111_01: Change the number on the right to match the number on the left.
UI_GUILDMENU8X6_NEW_F111_02: <white>Submitting a wrong answer increases the input wait time.</white>
UI_GUILDMENU8X6_NEW_F116_16: 1) Activity points are <white>donated daily at 00:00</white>. 2) Donated contribution points become guild points at a <white>1:0.15 ratio</white>. Ex) <white>100</white> contribution points = <white>15</white> guild points (donations only in units of <white>100</white>) 3) Donated points <white>cannot be refunded</white>.
UI_GUILDMONEY_NEW_F0_16: <white>Guild funds are distributed according to donation ratio.</white> <orange>Using the Distribute button cannot be canceled or restored.</orange> <orange>Distribution is unavailable if guild funds are below 10,000,000 Lance.</orange>
UI_HALLOWEENEVENTUI_NEW_F0_01:
● Collect candy to obtain a Halloween Decoration Band. 
● 10 candies complete 1 Decoration Band. 
● Each completed band sends a special reward to your mailbox.
UI_HELP_NEW_F10_01: 1. Movement Move with the arrow keys <- ↑ -> ↓ in the desired direction, or right-click with the mouse. 2. Chat Press [ENTER] to open chat mode, then type your message.
UI_HELP_NEW_F10_02: 3. Picking Up Items Move the mouse over an item, and the pointer changes to a hand. Left-click to put it in your bag. You can pick up items one tile away, including diagonals.
UI_HELP_NEW_F11_01: 4. Using Items Right-click to use an item on yourself. You can also place items in item macros and use number keys (1-9).
UI_HELP_NEW_F11_02: To use an item on another target, left-click the item first, move the held item cursor to the target, and right-click. In towns, you cannot use harmful items such as explosives, bombs, or holy water.
UI_HELP_NEW_F12_01: 5. Equipping To equip an item from your bag, open the equipment window first. Left-click the item once to hold it with the mouse cursor.
UI_HELP_NEW_F12_02: Place the held item in the equipment window and left-click to equip it.
UI_HELP_NEW_F13_01: 6. Giving Items Left-click an item to hold it, move it to the target character, and left-click. For trades, always use the exchange window to prevent scams.
UI_HELP_NEW_F13_02: 7. Attacking Stand next to a monster and press Space, or move the mouse pointer to the target and left-click. You cannot attack in towns.
UI_HELP_NEW_F14_01: To increase parameters, go to the guild for your job and click the guild keeper.
UI_HELP_NEW_F14_02: To increase 1 point, you need 1 Strength Orb up to 15, 3 up to 20, and 4 afterward.
UI_HELP_NEW_F15_01: STR: Limits equipment weight. INT: Increases holy resistance. AGR: Increases evasion (DC) and ice resistance. DEX: Increases accuracy (HC) and fire resistance. VIT: Increases defense (AC) and disease resistance. MEN: Increases attack (WC) and neutral resistance.
UI_HELP_NEW_F15_02: WC (Attack): Maximum attack power HC (Accuracy): Chance to hit DC (Evasion): Chance to avoid attacks AC (Defense): Reduces damage
UI_HELP_NEW_F16_01: Each job has its own skills. To raise skills, go to the relevant guild, click the guild keeper, and select the skill level. Raising skills uses Strength Orbs. Skill level attempts can succeed or fail.
UI_HELP_NEW_F16_02: As skill level rises, the chance of Strength Orb failure also increases. Use orbs wisely between parameters and skills.
UI_HELP_NEW_F17_01: Each time you level up, you receive a certain number based on your level. Use these Strength Orbs to raise base parameters or skill levels.
UI_HELP_NEW_F17_02: They cannot be obtained except by leveling up, so use them carefully. It is important to distribute them between parameters and skills.
UI_HELP_NEW_F18_01: Use the Steam Overlay F12 key to save screenshots.
UI_HELP_NEW_F19_01: When you first enter the game, you cannot leave town. To go outside town, train to level 6 at the training ground next to the barracks.
UI_HELP_NEW_F20_01: At level 6, you automatically move to the center of town. You are now ready to explore the continent of Godius. Continue along the central road.
UI_HELP_NEW_F20_02: Guards always stand at map exits. Move in front of a guard to automatically change locations. Enjoy your journey.

UI_INSTANCEDUNGEONUI_NEW_F0_05: * Entry count and ticket checks apply individually.
UI_INSTANCEDUNGEONUI_NEW_F0_06: * Each Matrix uses a different ticket. Please confirm you have the ticket for the selected Matrix.
UI_INSTANCEDUNGEONUI_NEW_F0_07: * Reward clay tablets are sent by mail.
UI_INSTANCEDUNGEONUI_NEW_F20_05: * No ticket required once per day (except boss battles)
UI_INSTANCEDUNGEONUI_NEW_F20_06: * No limit when using tickets (except boss battles)
UI_INSTANCEDUNGEONUI_NEW_F20_07: * Boss battles require a ticket (once per day)
UI_INSTANCEDUNGEONUI_NEW_F35_08: Requires the following Moonja Clay Tablets
UI_INSTANCEDUNGEONUI_NEW_F5_04: * Free entry once per day (no ticket required)
UI_INSTANCEDUNGEONUI_NEW_F5_05: * No entry limit when using tickets
UI_INSTANCEDUNGEONUI_NEW_F5_06: * Ticket checks and use apply per character
UI_KOREANNEWYEAREVENT_NEW_F0_01: It is the 2022 New Year, and I, Master Gat, would like some rice cake soup. Could you make rice cake soup for me? To make it, you need rice cakes, egg garnish, dumplings, dried meat, and broth. You can buy broth at the general shop, and obtain the other ingredients by hunting. Bring all the ingredients, and I will give you a lucky pouch for the New Year. I do not know what will come out. Happy New Year to everyone.
UI_MARKET_NEW_F107_04:
* Check item information before buying.
* Completed trades cannot be canceled.
* The marketplace can only use bank accounts.
* Purchased items are sent by mail.
* Pressing Buy means you agree to this notice.
UI_MARKET_NEW_F121_05: * The listing fee is withdrawn from your bank account when listed. * A fee is deducted from the sale price when sold. * Completed trades cannot be canceled. <orange>* The marketplace can only use bank accounts.</orange> <orange>* Pressing List means you agree to this notice.</orange>
UI_NEWCHARMENU8X6_NEW_F0_16: You can still adjust stats after auto setup. Blue stats are important for your main job. Values cannot be lower than the red numbers.
UI_NEWYEARGIFTEXCHANGE_NEW_F0_03: To welcome 2022, the Year of the Tiger, I prepared cereal to gather new strength, but monsters stole it overnight! At this rate, I cannot feel the tiger's energy!! Bring me cereal, milk from the general shop, and 2 Sardonyx, and I will give you a hidden gift! Sorry, but I have not opened those boxes either, so I do not know what is inside!
UI_NEWYEARGIFTEXCHANGE_NEW_F3_03: * You can exchange repeatedly during the event period. * Materials are consumed on exchange and cannot be restored. * Exchange is unavailable if materials are insufficient. * Exchange is unavailable if you do not have enough space.
UI_PLAYMENU8X6_NEW_F360_01: Will you inherit the <white>honor</white> and <white>blessing</white> of the <white>God of Godius</white> and convert to the new <white>growth</white> system <white>[Age]</white>?
UI_PLAYMENU8X6_NEW_F376_01: <white>Portal travel</white> to <white>Wellin Port</white> requires the following <white>cost</white>. Pay the <white>cost</white> and <white>move</white>?
UI_PLAYMENU8X6_NEW_F377_03: *Character name changes <white>apply immediately</white> and <white>cannot be reverted</white>. *After the change is complete, the screen <white>switches</white> to the <white>character select screen</white> to <white>apply</white> it.
UI_PLAYMENU8X6_NEW_F384_04: Imprint temporarily <white>grants the stats of a material accessory</white> to the target accessory. Even if the imprint effect <white>expires</white>, <white>the target item's normal effects still work</white>. Stats gained by imprint have <white>no set effect</white>. When imprinting is complete, <white>imprint materials are consumed</white> and cannot be restored.
UI_PLAYMENU8X6_NEW_F385_03: *Extends the <white>active imprint effect</white> of an imprinted accessory. *Only accessories with <white>expired imprint effects</white> can be registered. *Extending an imprint effect activates it for <white>28 days</white>.

UI_THANKSGIVINGDAY_NEW_F0_01: I am making Songpyeon, but I do not have enough ingredients. You can get <white>rice cake dough</white> from monsters in <white>normal hunting grounds</white>, and buy <white>Songpyeon filling at the general shop</white>. Bring <white>both ingredients</white> and place them in the mortar, and I will reward you with a <white>Strength Orb</white>. I do not have many Strength Orbs, so I can <white>reward you only once</white>.
UI_THANKSGIVINGDAY_NEW_F3_01: I am making Songpyeon, but I do not have enough ingredients. You can get <white>rice cake dough</white> from monsters in <white>normal hunting grounds</white>, and buy <white>Songpyeon filling at the general shop</white>. Bring <white>both ingredients</white> and place them in the mortar, and I can make Songpyeon. For <white>those who help a lot</white>, I will lend <white>the Nine-tailed Fox Orb</white> that I stole.
UI_THANKSGIVINGDAY_NEW_F4_01: Collect <white>Songpyeon</white> to obtain a <white>Chuseok Pattern</white>. <white>10</white> Songpyeon complete <white>1 Pattern</white>. Each completed pattern delivers a <white>special reward</white>.
